name: Inspector attachments upload

heading: CHEDD.GB.2026.1525824 - V1 Documents
bodyCopy: (file sub-page) file type must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS
(file sub-page) ZIP files are not allowed for security reasons
secondaryActions: Save and set as in progress

structure.text: New — Save and set as in progress (decision status bar above main)
CHEDD.GB.2026.1525824 - V1 Documents (reference-number span acts as caption, 'Documents' is the heading text)
Importer
1 additional document

Existing importer additional documents — columns: Document type / Document reference / Date of issue / Attachments. Row: Health certificate / REF-124 / 4 March 2023 / View example.png / Download. Attachment links take the form 'View {filename}'.

Container (#inspector-documents-table) wrapping the Inspector section: the list of documents the INSPECTOR has added, AND the add-document form beneath it. Distinct from the importer additional-documents grid above. RENDERED in this trace after the inspector uploaded example.jpg — showed a document row 'Other / INS-UPLOAD / 16 July 2026 / View example.jpg' with Download and Remove attachment controls (attachment link class 'govuk-link ellipsis').
structure.evidence: trace 0b4dcd0dec159e7c94583f4737ecaa8c4949acb9 action 122 (after snapshot: #inspector-documents-table renders link 'View example.jpg' + 'Download example.jpg' + 'Remove attachment {id}'). Corroborated by page-objects/notification/AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:35-37 (locatorInspectorAttachmentLink scopes 'View {filename}' to #inspector-documents-table).

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: defra-datepicker / date-picker__container / date-picker__dialog / date-picker__reveal__icon / date-picker__button__previous-month / date-picker__button__next-month / date-picker__button__close / date-picker__date-table / date-picker__heading / date-input--picker / date-picker-day / date-picker-month / date-picker-year
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke DEFRA calendar date-picker widget layered over the standard govuk-date-input. Adds a 'Choose date' reveal button that opens a dialog with previous/next-month navigation, a month heading (e.g. 'July 2026'), a selectable calendar table and a Cancel button, plus an aria-live-message region for screen readers.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: This is custom JS + CSS, not a GOV.UK Design System component. The Design System deliberately ships only the manual Day/Month/Year date input and warns against calendar pickers for known dates. The new app should default to the plain govuk-date-input (which is already present underneath) and drop the custom picker unless a strong UR need is proven.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-date-input (Date input) — already rendered here; use it without the overlay picker
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 0b4dcd0dec159e7c94583f4737ecaa8c4949acb9 action 114 (class inventory + structure outline). Corroborated by AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:15-24 which drives the plain Day/Month/Year textboxes only — the tests never touch the calendar picker, i.e. the manual input alone is sufficient to complete the journey.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: additional-documents-table / additional-documents__grid-row / additional-documents__grid-column-15 / additional-documents__grid-column-35 / additional-documents__grid-column-doc-date / additional-documents__headers / additional-documents__table-header / additional-documents__content-heading / additional-documents__content-value / additional-documents__content-links / additional-documents__date-of-issue-hint / additional-document-info / additional-document-content / add-document-attachment
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke CSS-grid layout used to render the list of existing (Importer) additional documents with columns Document type / Document reference / Date of issue / Attachments, and to lay out the Inspector add-document form row (select, reference, date, attach).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Hand-rolled grid classes instead of the GOV.UK Table (or Summary list) component. Reinvents column layout and header markup that the Design System provides, and mixes display concerns into custom classes. The rebuild should use govuk-table for the read-only document list and standard form-group stacking for the inspector inputs.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-table (Table) for the document list; Summary list is an alternative for a single record

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: link-button / no-wrap (on <button id="add-attachment"> 'Add attachment') / link-button (on <button id="button-add-additional-document"> 'Add a document') / link-button text-align-left (on the per-row 'Remove attachment {id}' button)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Submit buttons styled to look like text links ('Add attachment', 'Add a document', 'Remove attachment {id}'). 'Add attachment' POSTs and navigates to the separate file-upload sub-page; 'Add a document' adds another inspector document row; 'Remove attachment {id}' removes a saved inspector attachment. Only 'Add attachment' carries the extra 'no-wrap' class; 'Add a document' has plain 'link-button'; 'Remove attachment' has 'link-button text-align-left'.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a Design System pattern — a <button> restyled as a link via custom CSS. GOV.UK guidance is to use a real button (optionally govuk-button--secondary) for an action, or a genuine link for navigation, not a button disguised as a link. Accessibility/consistency risk.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-button with govuk-button--secondary, or a standard link if it is pure navigation
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 0b4dcd0dec159e7c94583f4737ecaa8c4949acb9 action 119 (button resolved to class 'link-button no-wrap'). 'Add attachment' role/label corroborated by AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:41-43 (btnAddAttachment = getByRole('button', { name: 'Add attachment' })).

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Assorted bespoke utility classes: a custom tertiary heading style, a narrow date-input wrapper, text-truncation, a screen-reader-only helper (duplicating govuk-visually-hidden), and an aria-live status region for the date picker.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom utilities that duplicate Design System equivalents (e.g. sr-only vs govuk-visually-hidden, heading-tertiary vs govuk-heading-s). Extra CSS surface to maintain; the rebuild should use the govuk equivalents.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-visually-hidden (for sr-only), govuk-heading-s/m (for heading-tertiary), govuk utility width classes (for short-date-input)

fields.name: document-type
fields.label: Document type
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy (CONDITIONAL): mandatory ONLY when a document row is being added. When the inspector/notifier fills any of type/reference/date OR clicks 'Add a document'/'Add attachment', the document-type field is validated as Joi.string().required().valid(...documentTypeMap keys). Blank -> error 'Select a document type'. The accompanying-documents SECTION itself remains optional (Save and continue with zero documents is allowed). Cite ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/documents_validation.js:44-52 (`.required()`, shouldValidateAdditionalDocument at :30-33) + messages/en.js:71 (DOCUMENT_TYPE = 'Select a document type'). Legacy policy — the rebuild may revisit whether all three row fields must be mandatory together.
fields.evidence: trace 0b4dcd0dec159e7c94583f4737ecaa8c4949acb9 action 114 (a11y tree + select options). Label is a column header ('Document type'), not a govuk-label; no HTML required attr but first option is the placeholder 'Select document type'. Corroborated by AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:8-10 (getByLabel('Document type').selectOption). Tests select 'Health certificate' (accessibility spec:70) and 'Other' (document-workflows.ts:8, inspectorFillsDocumentDetailsAndAddsAttachment). NOTE: the shared domain vocabulary types/document-type.ts lists ~27 document types across all CHED types; the 14 rendered here are the CHED-D/decision-side subset — treat the rendered 14 as the CHED-D option set, not the full domain.

fields.name: document-reference
fields.label: Document reference
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy (CONDITIONAL): mandatory when a document row is being added (same shouldValidateAdditionalDocument gate as document-type). For CHED-D (isChedp=false) the reference is Joi.string().required().max(100). Blank -> 'Enter a reference'. Cite documents_validation.js:53 + getReferenceValidation at :123-129. (Contrast: CHED-PP makes reference optional — Joi.string().optional().allow('').) Legacy policy the rebuild may revisit.
fields.maxLengthEvidence: legacy: Joi.string().max(100). Over 100 chars -> error 'Document reference must be 100 characters or fewer'. Cite documents_validation.js:125-128 + messages/en.js:3-5 (maxLengthMessage) + labels/en.js:44 (DOCUMENT_REFERENCE = 'Document reference'). This RESOLVES the prior 'no maxlength observed' gap.
fields.evidence: trace 0b4dcd0dec159e7c94583f4737ecaa8c4949acb9 action 115 (input id=document-reference, aria-label 'Document reference', title 'Documents'). Corroborated by AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:12-14 (getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Document reference' })); test values 'INS-UPLOAD' (document-workflows.ts:9), 'REF-124' (accessibility spec:71), 'INS-IMPORT' (document-workflows.ts:37). Maxlength/requiredness now sourced from legacy (see requiredEvidence/maxLengthEvidence) — NOTE these come from the notifier frontend (ipaffs-frontend-notification); the corpus trace captured the decision/BIP INSPECTOR side, a separate service, whose row-level copy may differ.

fields.name: document-issue-date-day / document-issue-date-month / document-issue-date-year
fields.label: Document issue date
fields.hint: Day Month Year (visible sub-labels; fieldset legend 'Document issue date' is visually hidden)
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy (CONDITIONAL): mandatory for CHED-D when a document row is being added — the handler calls dateValidation({... label: 'Date of issue', required: !isChedp}); for CHED-D isChedp=false so required=true (CHED-PP makes it optional). Blank -> 'Enter a date of issue'. Cite documents_validation.js:97-107 (required: !isChedp) + date_validator.js:99-105. Legacy policy the rebuild may revisit.
fields.evidence: trace 0b4dcd0dec159e7c94583f4737ecaa8c4949acb9 actions 116-118 (day/month/year textboxes) + action 114 (group 'Document issue date'). Accompanied by a custom 'Choose date' calendar picker. Corroborated by AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:16-49 (getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Day'/'Month'/'Year' }) + fillDocumentDate). Workflows fill a relative date via getRelativeDateTime() (document-workflows.ts:9,37) and a fixed 04/03/2023 in the accessibility spec:72. Missing/invalid-date validation copy now sourced from legacy (see validationMessages) — from the notifier frontend; inspector-side copy may differ.

fields.name: fileUpload
fields.label: Select a file
fields.hint: Your file must be: smaller than 10MB; larger than 1KB; file type must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS; ZIP files are not allowed for security reasons
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy: on the file-upload sub-page a file MUST be selected to proceed — empty filename -> error 'Select a file'. Cite ipaffs-frontend-upload/service/src/validation/file_upload_validation.js:87-90 (validate: `if (_.isEmpty(filename)) return FILE_NOT_SELECTED`) + utils/validation_constants.js:4 (FILE_NOT_SELECTED = 'Select a file').
fields.conditionalOn: Shown on the separate file-upload sub-page (/upload/.../{index}/decision/attach) reached by clicking 'Add attachment'
fields.allowedExtensionsEvidence: legacy: the AUTHORITATIVE server-side allow-list is doc, docx, jpg, jpeg, pdf, png, xlsx, xls. Cite ipaffs-frontend-upload/service/src/utils/validation_constants.js:19-23 (upload.accompanyingDocument.validationCriteria.ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS) + file_upload_validation.js:92-99 (extension lower-cased then checked against ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS). The on-screen guidance copy (ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS_FOR_MESSAGE) names only 'DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS' — i.e. DOCX and XLSX ARE accepted but not advertised (see discrepancies). Size bounds: max 10MB (10485760 bytes, TEN_MB) enforced via query param; min is 200 bytes (TWO_HUNDRED_B) in the single-upload validate(), NOT 1KB despite the '1KB' guidance copy (see discrepancies). Cite validation_constants.js:14-17 + file_upload_validation.js:106-108.
fields.evidence: trace 0b4dcd0dec159e7c94583f4737ecaa8c4949acb9 action 120 (set input files) + action 121 before (govuk-file-upload, input name=fileUpload, no accept attr — constraints shown as bullet list, validated server-side). Corroborated by DocumentUploadPage.ts (inputFile = locator('input[type="file"]')). VALID file types exercised by tests: jpg, png, doc, docx, xls, pdf (document-upload-valid-inspector.spec.ts:9) plus xlsx (document-workflows.ts:38) — matches the legacy allow-list above. INVALID types rejected: ZIP, TXT (document-upload-invalid-inspector.spec.ts:11-12). File-type filtering is entirely server-side (no HTML accept attr).

validationMessages.message: Your file must be: smaller than 10MB; larger than 1KB; file type must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS; ZIP files are not allowed for security reasons
validationMessages.trigger: Shown up-front as guidance on the file-upload sub-page; these are the file-size and file-type constraints. Displayed as a govuk-list--bullet, not as an error.
Submitting the file-upload sub-page with no file chosen. First check in the single-upload validator.
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-upload/service/src/validation/file_upload_validation.js:87-90 (`if (_.isEmpty(filename)) return FILE_NOT_SELECTED`) + utils/validation_constants.js:4 (FILE_NOT_SELECTED = 'Select a file'). Rendered in the error summary (role=alert).

validationMessages.message: The selected file must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS
validationMessages.trigger: Uploading a file whose (lower-cased) extension is not in the allow-list [doc, docx, jpg, jpeg, pdf, png, xlsx, xls]. Asserted by tests for .zip and .txt. Rendered in the error summary (role=alert) on the file-upload sub-page after clicking Next/Continue.
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-upload/service/src/validation/file_upload_validation.js:97-99 (util.format(FILE_TYPE_IS_INCORRECT_TEMPLATE, ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS_FOR_MESSAGE)) + utils/validation_constants.js:5,22 (template 'The selected file must be a %s'; message token 'DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS'). Corroborated by tests/document/document-upload-invalid-inspector.spec.ts:11-12,38-40 (asserts this string for example.zip and example.txt).

validationMessages.trigger: Uploading a zero-byte file (empty fileContent). Asserted by tests for empty-file.pdf. Rendered in the error summary (role=alert).
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-upload/service/src/validation/file_upload_validation.js:101-103 (`if (_.isEmpty(fileContent)) return FILE_IS_EMPTY`) + utils/validation_constants.js:8 (FILE_IS_EMPTY = 'The selected file is empty'). Corroborated by tests/document/document-upload-invalid-inspector.spec.ts:13,38-40 (empty-file.pdf).

validationMessages.message: The selected file must be larger than 1KB
validationMessages.trigger: Uploading a non-empty file smaller than 200 bytes (TWO_HUNDRED_B) in the single-upload validator. This is a DISTINCT under-size message, separate from 'The selected file is empty'. NOTE the threshold enforced by code is 200 bytes, not the 1KB the guidance copy and this message imply.
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-upload/service/src/validation/file_upload_validation.js:106-108 (`if (_.size(fileContent) < TWO_HUNDRED_B) return FILE_TOO_SMALL`) + utils/validation_constants.js:7 (FILE_TOO_SMALL = 'The selected file must be larger than 1KB'), :15 (TWO_HUNDRED_B = 200). NOT asserted by any CHED-D test (tests only cover empty-file, over-size, wrong-type) — new, from legacy source. Answers the prior open question about a distinct sub-1KB (non-empty) message.

validationMessages.message: The selected file must be smaller than 10MB
validationMessages.trigger: Uploading a file larger than 10MB (10485760 bytes). In the single-upload path this fires via the query-param guard (hapi rejects the over-size payload, sub-page re-renders with fileTooLarge=true). Asserted by tests for large-file.pdf. Rendered in the error summary (role=alert).
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-upload/service/src/validation/file_upload_validation.js:159-163 (validateQueryParams: fileTooLarge -> FILE_TOO_LARGE) + utils/validation_constants.js:6 (FILE_TOO_LARGE = 'The selected file must be smaller than 10MB'), :16 (TEN_MB = 10485760). Corroborated by tests/document/document-upload-invalid-inspector.spec.ts:14,38-40 (large-file.pdf).

validationMessages.trigger: Adding a document row (any of type/reference/date filled OR 'Add a document'/'Add attachment' clicked) with Document type left as the placeholder. Document type is Joi.required() + must be a valid documentTypeMap key. NOTE: sourced from the notifier frontend (ipaffs-frontend-notification); the corpus trace captured the decision/BIP INSPECTOR side, a separate service, whose copy may differ.
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/documents_validation.js:44-52 (`.required()`; shouldValidateAdditionalDocument :30-33) + messages/en.js:71 (DOCUMENT_TYPE = 'Select a document type'). Resolves the prior GAP on blank-row validation.

validationMessages.trigger: Adding a document row (CHED-D) with Document reference blank. For CHED-D the reference is Joi.required() (CHED-PP makes it optional). Notifier-frontend copy; inspector-side may differ.
validationMessages.evidence: documents_validation.js:53,123-129 (getReferenceValidation: non-CHED-PP -> Joi.string().required()) + messages/en.js:31-33 (requiredMessage(label,article) = `Enter ${article} ${label.toLowerCase()}`) + labels/en.js:45 (REFERENCE = 'Reference'), :2 (A = 'a') -> 'Enter a reference'.

validationMessages.message: Document reference must be 100 characters or fewer
validationMessages.trigger: Document reference longer than 100 characters. Joi.string().max(100).
validationMessages.evidence: documents_validation.js:125-128 (.max(100), STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(DOCUMENT_REFERENCE, 100)) + messages/en.js:3-5 (maxLengthMessage = `${label} must be ${maxLength} characters or fewer`) + labels/en.js:44 (DOCUMENT_REFERENCE = 'Document reference'). Resolves the prior maxlength GAP.

validationMessages.trigger: Adding a document row (CHED-D) with the whole Date of issue (day+month+year) blank. Date is required for CHED-D (required: !isChedp). Notifier-frontend copy; inspector-side may differ.
validationMessages.evidence: documents_validation.js:97-107 (dateValidation label 'Date of issue', required: !isChedp) + date_validator.js:99-105 (validateRequiredFields -> DATE_MISSING_ERROR) + validation_messages.js:13 (DATE_MISSING_ERROR = `Enter a ${label.toLowerCase()}`) -> 'Enter a date of issue'. Resolves prior date GAP.

validationMessages.message: Date of issue must include a day | ...include a month | ...include a year | ...include a day and month | ...include a month and year | ...include a day and year
validationMessages.trigger: Partially-completed Date of issue (one or two of day/month/year missing). The specific message names exactly which parts are missing.
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:108-127 (validateIncompleteness) + validation_messages.js:14-19 (DAY_MISSING_ERROR/MONTH_MISSING_ERROR/YEAR_MISSING_ERROR/DAY_AND_MONTH_MISSING_ERROR/MONTH_AND_YEAR_MISSING_ERROR/DAY_AND_YEAR_MISSING_ERROR, each `${label} must include a ...`).

validationMessages.message: Day must be from 1 to 31 | Month must be from 1 to 12 | Year must be 4 numbers | Year must be later than 1969
validationMessages.trigger: Out-of-range date components: day outside 1-31, month outside 1-12, year not 4 digits, or year <= 1969.
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:129-172 (validateDateRange/validateYear) + validation_messages.js:20-22 (RANGE_VALIDATION_ERROR = `${label} must be from ${min} to ${max}`; MAX_YEAR_LENGTH_VALIDATION_ERROR = `${label} must be 4 numbers`; MIN_YEAR_VALIDATION_ERROR = `${label} must be later than ${minYear}`) + validation_constants.js:327-331 (MIN_DAY 1, MAX_DAY 31, MIN_MONTH 1, MAX_MONTH 12, MIN_YEAR 1969).

validationMessages.message: Date of issue must be a real date
validationMessages.evidence: date_validator.js:174-181 (validateValidDate -> INVALID_DATE_ERROR) + validation_messages.js:23 (INVALID_DATE_ERROR = `${label} must be a real date`).

openQuestions: SURFACE SCOPE CORRECTION: this 'Accompanying Documents' page is NOT inspector-only. The identical page object (pages.accompanyingDocuments) is driven in the MAIN notifier create-notification journey too, positioned between 'Commodity Additional Details' and 'Traders Addresses' (tests/accessibility/ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:69-83). Our corpus trace happened to capture it from the decision/BIP inspector side (/decision/.../{ched}/documents), but the notifier fills the same document-type/reference/date + Add attachment flow mid-journey. The slug 'inspector-attachments-upload' and journeyPosition -1 understate this — the page belongs in the main journey AND is re-used on the inspector side. Confirm the rebuild treats it as one shared 'accompanying documents' capability across both surfaces.

RESOLVED (legacy): file-error copy is now sourced from IPAFFS ipaffs-frontend-upload (validation_constants.js + file_upload_validation.js), not merely inferred from tests. Five single-upload messages apply: 'Select a file' (no file), 'The selected file must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS' (wrong type), 'The selected file is empty' (zero bytes), 'The selected file must be larger than 1KB' (non-empty but <200 bytes — the distinct sub-1KB message that was previously an open question DOES exist), 'The selected file must be smaller than 10MB' (>10MB, via query-param guard). Confirm these still match the live error-summary copy for the rebuild.

LEGACY DISCREPANCY (copy vs enforced threshold): the on-screen guidance bullet and the FILE_TOO_SMALL message both say 'larger than 1KB', but the single-upload validator enforces a 200-byte floor (TWO_HUNDRED_B = 200), not 1000 bytes. (The multiUpload path DOES use MIN_FILE_LENGTH_BYTES = 1000.) Decide the intended minimum-size rule for the rebuild and make the copy match the code. Cite validation_constants.js:15,99 + file_upload_validation.js:106-108,152.

ACCEPTED FILE-TYPE DISCREPANCY (now legacy-confirmed): the AUTHORITATIVE server-side allow-list is [doc, docx, jpg, jpeg, pdf, png, xlsx, xls] (validation_constants.js:21), but the on-screen guidance copy names only 'DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS' (validation_constants.js:22) — so DOCX and XLSX are accepted but not advertised. This is confirmed in legacy source, not just test-inferred. Confirm the authoritative accepted-extension list for the new app and fix the guidance copy to match.

The file input has no 'accept' attribute, so file-type filtering is entirely server-side. Confirm the allowed-type list (DOC, DOCX, JPEG, PDF, PNG, XLS, XLSX) and the size bounds (empty/1KB lower, 10MB upper) are the current business rule for the new app.

RESOLVED (legacy, with a caveat): missing-required-field validation on the document row is NOT a gap in the notifier frontend. For CHED-D, when a row is started, all three are mandatory — document-type ('Select a document type'), document-reference ('Enter a reference', max 100 chars 'Document reference must be 100 characters or fewer'), and Date of issue ('Enter a date of issue' + the granular day/month/year/range/real-date messages). Cite documents_validation.js:44-107. CAVEAT: this copy comes from ipaffs-frontend-notification (the notifier journey), whereas the corpus trace captured the decision/BIP INSPECTOR side (a separate service, ipaffs-frontend-decision/ipaffs-decision-microservice). Confirm the inspector-side row validation copy matches, or capture it separately. Also note mandatoriness is CONDITIONAL — the accompanying-documents section itself is optional (you can Save and continue with zero documents); the three fields become mandatory only once a row is being added. This is legacy policy the rebuild may revisit.

The Importer-vs-Inspector split shows TWO document collections on one page: a read-only importer additional-documents grid (with 'View {filename}' links) plus an editable inspector section (#inspector-documents-table) whose saved documents render as rows above the add-document form. CONFIRMED rendered in this trace at action 122 (after upload): row 'Other / INS-UPLOAD / 16 July 2026 / View example.jpg' with Download and a 'Remove attachment {id}' button. Confirm whether the new simple app keeps this dual-collection model or unifies them.

A 'Success' region/banner (getByRole('region', { name: 'Success' }), AccompanyingDocumentsPage.ts:26-28) is shown after an attachment is added, but was never rendered in our corpus trace. Confirm its copy/placement.

The file-upload sub-page primary button is 'Next' in our trace but the page object matches /^(Next|Continue)$/ (DocumentUploadPage.ts:btnNextOrContinue) — the label varies by context (Next vs Continue). Confirm the intended single label for the rebuild.

Custom defra-datepicker overlays the govuk-date-input — the tests only ever drive the plain Day/Month/Year textboxes, so the manual input alone is sufficient. Recommend dropping the custom picker and using the plain manual date input; needs UR/product sign-off.

RESOLVED (legacy): Document reference maxlength is 100 characters (Joi.string().max(100), documents_validation.js:125-128); over-length copy 'Document reference must be 100 characters or fewer'. No format/pattern constraint beyond that.
